reasoning.typography: Geist typeface has character but execution is safe — clean sans-serif with decent weight variation. The testimonial section shows bold logo names that do typographic work, but overall system lacks risk-taking.
reasoning.hierarchy: Strong hero dominance with gradient triangle asset commanding attention. Headline sizing works well, testimonial metrics (7m→40s, 95%, 24x) are visually loud in the right way. Clear primary action hierarchy.
reasoning.distinctiveness: The gradient triangle is a strong brand asset, but the IA structure (hero → social proof → product cards) is developer-tools-stencil. Swap 'Vercel' for 'Netlify' and 90% still works. Testimonial presentation has some personality.
reasoning.color: Disciplined dark theme with teal-to-red gradient used as singular accent moment. White text, dark cards, restrained palette. Gradient feels earned as centerpiece, not decorative. Small blue event badge controlled.
reasoning.density: Confident spacing in hero area with breathing room around headline. Card grid below has good rhythm. Not maximalist but not over-padded SaaS either. Slightly generic uniform card heights.
reasoning.voice: Structure is pure B2B dev-tools template: hero CTA → social proof logos → filtered product cards → feature grid. The 'AI Cloud' positioning is clear but IA doesn't break from category conventions. Reads professional, not visionary.

winning_patterns: Gradient triangle as singular focal asset — gives the page a memorable centerpiece
Testimonial metrics styled as bold typographic statements (runway/LEONARDO.AI/zapier + numbers)
Dark theme executed with restraint — not trying to be 'cool dark mode'
Hero headline does the positioning work clearly: 'Build and deploy on the AI Cloud'
Tab filtering interface (AI Apps/Web Apps/Ecommerce) shows product range efficiently
anti_patterns: IA is pure category template: hero → proof → cards → features — could be any dev platform
Product cards ('Your product, delivered' / 'Agents' / 'AI Apps') feel generic in phrasing
Event banner at top ('Ship 26 coming to 5 cities') breaks the hero's visual authority
Card descriptions too vague ('Deliver more value to users by executing complex workflows')
Footer card grid spacing feels mechanically uniform — no density variation or visual surprise
_note: Bold mono typography, gradient hero, motion accents
